skill.name: storage-management
skill.description: Manage storage devices and resources in Proxmox. Monitor storage usage, allocate resources, and plan storage expansion for your virtualization infrastructure.
skill.summary: Review Proxmox storage capacity, utilization, allocation, and expansion needs.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown documentation and tool names. No executable code, prompt injection, network behavior, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Tool Names Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are Markdown references to get_storage and get_node_storage workflows. They do not contain Ruby backticks, shell commands, dynamic execution, or user-controlled command arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows Markdown list items and numbered workflow steps. No executable context or command invocation syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Plain Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are descriptive prose about storage devices and expansion planning. They do not define or call DES, MD5, RC4, SHA1, or another weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain Markdown text in the skill description and workflow. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive implementation exists.

content.value_statement: Proxmox teams need clear storage visibility before capacity problems affect workloads. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code review storage usage, node allocation, and expansion needs.
actual_capabilities: Lists storage devices available across a Proxmox cluster.
Reviews storage devices for a specific Proxmox node.
Summarizes capacity, utilization, and available space.
Supports storage planning conversations for growth and upgrades.
Helps identify underused, full, or potentially constrained storage areas.
Frames storage reports for VM and container infrastructure reviews.
limitations: It depends on available Proxmox storage tool responses.
It does not directly modify storage pools or delete data.
It does not replace vendor health checks for disks, RAID, or controllers.
Trend analysis needs historical data from the connected environment.

use_cases.title: Check Cluster Capacity
use_cases.description: Review total storage, free space, and high-utilization locations before adding workloads.

use_cases.title: Plan Storage Expansion
use_cases.description: Compare current utilization with expected growth and prepare upgrade recommendations.

use_cases.title: Investigate Node Storage Issues
use_cases.description: Inspect node-level storage details when a VM, container, or backup job reports space problems.

prompt_templates.title: Show Storage Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all Proxmox storage devices with capacity, used space, free space, and utilization percentage.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Node
prompt_templates.prompt: Review storage for node NODE_NAME and highlight any storage areas near capacity.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Capacity Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze current Proxmox storage utilization and create a capacity planning summary for the next quarter.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Storage Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare cluster and node storage data, identify bottlenecks, and recommend prioritized actions for reliability and growth.

output_examples.input: Show me all storage devices and their capacity.
output: A cluster storage summary grouped by storage device.
Capacity, used space, free space, and utilization status.
A short list of storage areas that need attention.

output_examples.input: What is the storage usage per node?
output: Capacity pressure indicators for each node.
Recommended follow-up checks for high-utilization nodes.
A capacity planning summary with current limits.
Likely expansion priorities based on utilization.
Operational recommendations for redundancy and monitoring.

best_practices: Keep enough free space for snapshots, backups, and workload growth.
Review node-level storage before moving or creating large workloads.
Combine utilization data with hardware health checks before expansion decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not wait until storage is full before planning expansion.
Do not treat storage capacity data as a replacement for backups.
Do not rebalance or delete data without confirming workload impact.

faq.question: What Proxmox storage tasks does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports storage listing, node storage review, utilization summaries, capacity planning, and operational reporting.

faq.question: Can it change storage configuration?
faq.answer: No. The documented skill focuses on analysis and reporting, not direct storage modification.

faq.question: Does it work across a whole cluster?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes cluster-wide storage review and node-specific storage analysis workflows.

faq.question: Can it find storage bottlenecks?
faq.answer: It can help identify capacity pressure and usage imbalance when the required storage data is available.

faq.question: Does it verify physical disk health?
faq.answer: No. Disk, RAID, and controller health should be checked with the proper Proxmox or vendor tools.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
